itineraries.name: Route des Vins three-day north-south traverse
itineraries.theme: The full Alsace Wine Route from Marlenheim down to Thann, with grand cru villages and a Strasbourg gateway

days.morning: Drive west from Strasbourg through Marlenheim and start the wine route proper. Mid-morning visit at a northern Bas-Rhin estate such as Domaine Pfister in Dahlenheim, on the Engelberg Grand Cru slope.
days.lunch: Lunch in Obernai or Barr on the wine route.
days.afternoon: Afternoon at Domaine Stoeffler in Barr for the Kirchberg de Barr Grand Cru, including the 2022-eligible Pinot Noir.
days.evening: Drive south to Ribeauville for an evening glass at a grower wine bar.

days.morning: Morning at Trimbach in Ribeauville for the house Riesling lineup, then a short drive to Hunawihr.
days.afternoon: Afternoon at Hugel in Riquewihr followed by Domaine Mittnacht Freres in Hunawihr, comparing big-name and biodynamic-grower styles.

days.morning: Drive south to Turckheim for a morning at Domaine Zind-Humbrecht, a long-running biodynamic estate working Riesling parcel by parcel.
days.afternoon: Afternoon at Domaine Weinbach in Kaysersberg followed by a stop at Domaines Schlumberger in Guebwiller for the four-Grand-Cru lineup.
days.evening: Final dinner in Colmar and a stroll through Petite Venise.

itineraries.description: A three-day traverse of the Alsace Wine Route from Strasbourg's northern doorstep down to Guebwiller in the south, hitting Grand Cru villages day by day.
itineraries.tip: Reserve domaine appointments weeks ahead, especially for Trimbach and Zind-Humbrecht; the route between villages is short but the cellar bookings are not.

itineraries.name: Grand Cru deep dive: Ribeauville to Riquewihr in two days
itineraries.theme: The heart of the Haut-Rhin Grand Cru belt, hitting Geisberg, Schoenenbourg, Rosacker and Kaefferkopf

days.morning: Start in Ribeauville with a morning at Trimbach for the Cuvee Frederic Emile Riesling from Geisberg and Osterberg.
days.afternoon: Afternoon at Domaine Mittnacht Freres in Hunawihr for the Rosacker Grand Cru, then a walk up to the Rosacker slope and stork sanctuary.
days.evening: Dinner in Ribeauville with a Riesling Grand Cru pour by the glass.

days.morning: Drive south to Riquewihr for a morning at Hugel et Fils, with a tasting of the Schoenenbourg Grand Cru Riesling.
days.afternoon: Drive across to Ammerschwihr for an afternoon at Domaine Geschickt in the Kaefferkopf Grand Cru, with a side-by-side of skin-contact and conventional cuvees.
days.evening: Return to Ribeauville for the final night.

itineraries.description: A two-day Grand Cru route through Ribeauville, Hunawihr, Riquewihr and Ammerschwihr, comparing Geisberg, Rosacker, Schoenenbourg and Kaefferkopf side by side.
itineraries.tip: The four villages sit within a 12-kilometre triangle; pick one base and treat the tour as a series of half-day cellar runs rather than a long drive each day.

itineraries.name: Strasbourg to the vineyards: one-day taster
itineraries.theme: A single day pairing Strasbourg's wine culture with a half-day on the northern wine route

days.morning: Begin at the Cave Historique des Hospices de Strasbourg by the old hospital, the cellar that holds the 1472 cask and bottles 30-plus Alsatian estates' wines under a single label.
days.afternoon: Drive 25 minutes west to Dahlenheim for an afternoon at Domaine Pfister on the Engelberg Grand Cru, then a short tasting at Domaine Stoeffler in Barr if time allows.
days.evening: Return to Strasbourg for a wine-bar dinner.

itineraries.description: A one-day taste of Alsace from Strasbourg, pairing the 1472-cask hospital cellar with a northern wine-route afternoon at Dahlenheim and Barr.
itineraries.tip: Strasbourg sits 25 to 40 minutes from the northern wine route; this day suits travellers with limited time who still want to taste at the vineyard, not just in town.

itineraries.name: Biodynamic and natural-wine deep dive, two days
itineraries.theme: Established Alsatian biodynamic houses and the new natural-wine wave

days.morning: Morning at Domaine Zind-Humbrecht in Turckheim, a long-running biodynamic estate working Grand Cru Riesling and Pinot Gris.
days.afternoon: Afternoon at Domaine Marcel Deiss in Bergheim, Demeter biodynamic and known for permitted field-blend Grand Cru Altenberg de Bergheim.
days.evening: Dinner in Colmar with a natural-wine list.

days.morning: Drive to Scherwiller for a morning at Domaine Achillee, the Dietrich brothers' Demeter biodynamic natural-wine estate in a straw-bale cellar.
days.afternoon: Afternoon at Domaine Pierre Frick in Pfaffenheim, Biodyvin-certified and a long-running zero-sulphur producer, then a short stop at Domaine Geschickt in Ammerschwihr for skin-contact whites.

itineraries.description: A two-day route through Alsace's established biodynamic and natural-wine houses, from Demeter-certified Zind-Humbrecht and Deiss to zero-sulphur Pierre Frick and Achillee.
itineraries.tip: Alsace has one of the highest concentrations of certified biodynamic estates in France; this route shows both the established names and the new natural-wine wave in two days.

itineraries.name: Wine and spirits combo, three days
itineraries.theme: Pair the Grand Cru wine route with Lapoutroie's eau-de-vie distilleries

days.morning: Morning at Trimbach in Ribeauville followed by a stop at Domaine Sylvie Spielmann in Bergheim for the Altenberg de Bergheim Grand Cru.
days.afternoon: Afternoon at Hugel et Fils in Riquewihr, then a stroll through the medieval centre.

days.morning: Drive west into the Vallee de Kaysersberg to Lapoutroie, the centre of Alsatian eau-de-vie distilling, for a morning at the Massenez or Miclo distillery.
days.lunch: Lunch in the Vallee de Kaysersberg.
days.afternoon: Afternoon at Domaine Weinbach in Kaysersberg-Vignoble for the Riesling and Pinot Gris Grand Cru lineup from the Schlossberg slope.

days.morning: Morning at Domaine Zind-Humbrecht in Turckheim for the Brand and Rangen de Thann Grand Cru wines.
days.afternoon: Afternoon at Domaines Schlumberger in Guebwiller for the four-Grand-Cru lineup and the estate's eau-de-vie of marc.

itineraries.description: A three-day combination of grand-cru wine days and the Vallee de Kaysersberg eau-de-vie distillery cluster, pairing the two great spirits of Alsace.
itineraries.tip: Eau-de-vie is the often-missed half of Alsatian drinks culture; book a Lapoutroie distillery visit alongside the wine cellars to taste the full range from Riesling to mirabelle and kirsch.
